content_en:
As for citing as the evidence the words of Ibn Al-Jawzi on this subject, we say: The sayings of the scholars may be cited as evidence or evidence may be cited against them, and the saying of one scholar is not an evidence against the rest of them.

As for their claim that Imam Ahmad made Ta‘wil in the Hadith:

WSN lal by gtd 35 6st Lg Syn

“Verily, the hearts ‘of the sons of Adam are between two of the Fingers of the Most Beneficent.” And the Hadith:

(oN Gb ail fa 55 Sed “The Black Stone is the right Hand of Allah in the earth.” and the Words of Allah, the Most High:

Sak ch Bee ae Ek CG KE Bs ‘And He is with you (by His Knowledge) wheresoever you may be. mee] We say: It has not been authentically reported from Imam Ahmad, may Allah have mercy on him, that he made Ta’wil of the above-mentioned two Ahadith.

Fatawa on ‘Aqgidah (Creed) 107 Beda! cul

Shaikhul-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah, may Allah have mercy on him, said in Al-Fatawa (5:398) in the collection of Ibn Qasim: As for what was reported by Abu Hamid Al-Ghazali, that Imam Ahmad did not make Ta’wil except in three things:

(250 pal asi te 351 el “The Black Stone is the right Hand of Allah in the earth.” and: tgAS3I lal by otel og oti Sth “The hearts of the sons of Adam are between two of the Fingers of the Most Beneficent.”

tad ds ell ons bei eo! “Verily, I find the Spirit of the Most Beneficent in the direction of Yemen.”

This report is a lie against Ahmad; no one has transmitted it from him with an authentic chain of narrators, and it is not known that any of his companions transmitted it from him.

As for the Words of Allah, the Most High:

‘And He is with you (by His nevi, wheresoever you may be. rl) Imam Ahmad did not make Ta’wil of it; he only explained it by one of its essential attributes, which is Knowledge, in reply to the Jahmiyyah,'?! who explained it in a manner which contradicted its true meaning, claiming that it necessitates the belief

() Al-Hadid 57:4 '?] Jahmiyyah: Named after its founder, Jahm bin Safwan, the Jahmiyyah movement denied all of the Names and Attributes of Allah.

that Allah, the Most High is in every place in His Person Exalted is Allah above what they claim. So he, may Allah have mercy on him, explained that His being with them here means that He encompasses the creation by His Knowledge of them. This is because being with something or someone does not necessitate its physical presence or mixing with it. Rather it must be understood in each place according to its context; this is why we say: He gave me milk with water to drink. And it is said: I prayed with the congregation. And it is said: So-and-so’s wife is with him. In the first example, associating and mixing is necessarily understood, while in the second example, sharing the same place and the same deed must be understood, without mixing; and in the third example, accompanying must be understood, even though they may not share the same place or action. Once it is clear that the meaning of being with something or someone differs according to what is annexed to it, and it is not possible to say that it necessitates associating and mixing, or sharing the same place, because it is impossible to attribute that to Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, due to the fact that it is confirmed that He is in no way similar to them and the fact that He is far above them. Therefore, He is with us and He is over His Throne, above the heavens, because He encompasses us with His Knowledge, His Omnipotence, His Authority, His Hearing, His Seeing and His Organizing the affairs of the creation and other matters necessitated by His Lordship. So, if a commentator explained it as meaning Knowledge, he has not exceeded what is necessitated by it and he is not thereby guilty of Ta‘wil, except, at any rate, to those who understand being with someone or something as meaning sharing the same place or associating and mixing. And we have already said that this is not necessarily so in all cases. This regards what has been transmitted from Imam Ahmad concerning the (supposed) Ta’wil of these three evidences.

Fatawa on ‘Agidah (Creed) 109 Badal! g glcd

As for looking at the texts themselves, we have mentioned above that there is no Ta’wil in the Noble Verse, if a commentator explained it as meaning knowledge, because that is a part of what must necessarily be understood by it, it is not a form of distorting its meaning from what must be understood by it.

As for the Hadith:

“Verily, the hearts of the sons of Adam are between two of the

Fingers of the Most Beneficent are like one heart and He does with

them as He wills.” It was narrated by Muslim in his Sahih, in the Book of Al-Qadar in the third Chapter, number 17, page 2045; and there is no Ta'wil in it, according to Ahl as-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah, since they confirm belief in the Fingers of Allah, the Most High in a manner befitting Him, and the fact that our hearts are between two Fingers does not necessitate that they are touching the hearts. This is because the clouds are held between the heavens and the earth and they neither touch the sky nor the earth; likewise, the hearts of the sons of Adam are between two Fingers of the Most Beneficent and it does not necessitate that they are touching them.

As for the Hadith: “The Black Stone is the right Hand of Allah in the earth.”

Shaikhul-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah said regarding it in Al-Fatawa, (6:397) in the compilation of Ibn Qasim: It has been narrated from the Prophet #¢ with an unauthentic chain of narrators and what is well known is that it is from Ibn ‘Abbas, may Allah be

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 110 PLY OS! sats

pleased with them, who said: ‘The Black Stone is the right Hand of Allah in the earth, so whoever touched it and kissed it, it is as if he had shaken Allah’s right Hand and kissed it.’ So he restricted it to being on earth and not in every place and he says: ‘the right Hand of Allah in the earth,’ and the ruling on a restricted wording is different from something general and unrestricted. And he said: ‘so whoever touched it and kissed it, it is as if he had shaken Allah’s right Hand and kissed it.’ And it is well known that being similar to something is not the same as being identical to it.

I say: Accordingly, the Hadith is not speaking of the Attributes of Allah, the Most High whose meanings have been changed from the apparent meanings, therefore there is no Ta’wil in it at all.

As for their claim that there are two schools: One of them known as the school of Ibn Taymiyyah, we say that the attribution of this school to Ibn Taymiyyah is in order to claim that no one before him said these things. This is wrong, because the beliefs held by Ibn Taymiyyah are those which were held by the righteous Salaf and the Imams of the (Muslim) nation, and it was not he who introduced this school, as claimed by the one who says this, and who wishes thereby to minimize its importance and Allah is the One Who is asked for Help.

As for our position regarding those scholars who made Ta’‘wil, we say: Any of them who was known to have a good intention and who contributed positively to the religion and who followed the Sunnah is excused, due to his having exercised Ta’wil for some reason (which he saw as justifiable). However, his being excused does not mean that his path was not wrong, that is the method contrary to that of the righteous Salaf which was to understand them according to their apparent meaning, and to believe what that apparent meaning proved, without trying to explain how, and without comparison.

It is necessary to differentiate between the ruling on a saying, and on the one who said it, and between a deed and the one who performed it. So, if an incorrect statement emanates from the exercise of Ijtihad and a good intention, then the one who said it is not blamed for it. Indeed, he has a reward for his Tjtihad, according to the statement of the Prophet #:

SESE 5S ty col sh AB oul SF aged ass (S&S tip

oi i Bi id “If a judge makes a ruling and he exercises Ijtihad (therein), and he is correct, then he has two rewards; but if he makes a ruling and exercise Ijtihad (therein) and he is wrong, then he has one reward.’"")

But as for him being described as astray, if what is meant by it is that he is completely astray, for which the person so described is held blameworthy and which is hated, then this description is not applied to this Mujtahid'?) who is known to have had a good intention, contributed positively to the religion, and followed the Sunnah.

But if what is intended by being astray is that his opinion contradicts the correct view, without indicating thereby censure of the one who holds it, then there is no objection to that, because such a person as this is not completely astray. Regarding his method, he is correct, since he has striven to the utmost of his ability to arrive at the truth, but as regards the result (of his Ijtihad) he is astray, since he has contradicted the truth.

"1 Reported by Ai-Bukhari in the Book of Holding Fast to the Book (of Allah) and the Sunnah, in the Chapter: The Reward of the Judge Who Exercises [jtihad and He is Correct or Wrong (7352) and by Muslim in the Book of Judgements, in the Chapter: Explanation of the Reward of the Judge When He Exercise Ijtihad (1716).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 112 PEM ols i cst

And by this detailed explanation, doubts and worries are removed. And Allah is the One Who is asked.

Q. 30. What is the belief of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah . regarding the Names and Attributes of Allah? And what is the difference between a Name and an Attribute? And does confirming the Name necessitate confirming the Attribute? And does confirming the Attribute necessitate confirming the Name?

A. The belief of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah regarding the Names and Attributes of Allah is to confirm the Names and Attributes which Allah has confirmed for Himself, without distorting the meaning, without negating it, with explaining how and without comparison.
content_id:
Adapun penggunaan perkataan Ibnu Al-Jawzi dalam masalah ini sebagai dalil, kami katakan: Perkataan para ulama boleh dijadikan dalil atau dalil boleh dikemukakan untuk membantah mereka, dan perkataan seorang ulama bukanlah hujjah terhadap ulama lainnya.

Adapun klaim mereka bahwa Imam Ahmad melakukan ta'wil dalam hadith:

WSN lal by gtd 35 6st Lg Syn

"Sesungguhnya hati anak-anak Adam berada di antara dua Jari dari jari-jari Yang Maha Pengasih." Dan hadith:

(oN Gb ail fa 55 Sed "Hajar Aswad adalah Tangan Kanan Allah di bumi." serta firman Allah Ta'ala:

Sak ch Bee ae Ek CG KE Bs 'Dan Dia bersama kamu (dengan ilmu-Nya) di mana pun kamu berada. mee] Kami katakan: Tidak ada riwayat yang sahih dari Imam Ahmad, semoga Allah merahmatinya, bahwa beliau melakukan ta'wil terhadap dua hadith yang disebutkan di atas.

Fatawa tentang Aqidah 107 Beda! cul

Syaikhul Islam Ibnu Taimiyyah, semoga Allah merahmatinya, berkata dalam Al-Fatawa (5:398) dalam kumpulan Ibnu Qasim: Adapun apa yang diriwayatkan oleh Abu Hamid Al-Ghazali, bahwa Imam Ahmad tidak melakukan ta'wil kecuali dalam tiga hal:

(250 pal asi te 351 el "Hajar Aswad adalah Tangan Kanan Allah di bumi." dan: tgAS3I lal by otel og oti Sth "Hati anak-anak Adam berada di antara dua Jari dari jari-jari Yang Maha Pengasih."

tad ds ell ons bei eo! "Sesungguhnya aku mendapati hembusan nafas Yang Maha Pengasih dari arah Yaman."

Riwayat ini adalah kedustaan atas nama Ahmad; tidak ada seorang pun yang meriwayatkannya darinya dengan sanad yang sahih, dan tidak diketahui bahwa ada seorang pun dari murid-muridnya yang meriwayatkannya darinya.

Adapun firman Allah Ta'ala:

'Dan Dia bersama kamu (dengan ilmu-Nya) di mana pun kamu berada. rl) Imam Ahmad tidak melakukan ta'wil terhadapnya; beliau hanya menjelaskannya dengan salah satu sifat yang menjadi keharusannya, yaitu ilmu, sebagai jawaban terhadap Jahmiyyah,'?! yang menjelaskannya dengan cara yang bertentangan dengan makna sebenarnya, dengan mengklaim bahwa ayat tersebut mengharuskan keyakinan

() Al-Hadid 57:4 '?] Jahmiyyah: Dinamai menurut pendirinya, Jahm bin Safwan, gerakan Jahmiyyah mengingkari semua Nama dan Sifat Allah.

bahwa Allah Ta'ala berada di setiap tempat dengan Dzat-Nya — Mahasuci Allah dari apa yang mereka klaim. Maka beliau, semoga Allah merahmatinya, menjelaskan bahwa kebersamaan-Nya dengan mereka di sini bermakna bahwa Dia meliputi makhluk dengan ilmu-Nya terhadap mereka. Ini karena kebersamaan dengan sesuatu atau seseorang tidak mengharuskan adanya kehadiran fisik atau bercampur dengannya. Bahkan ia harus dipahami di setiap konteks sesuai dengan tempatnya; oleh karena itu kita berkata: Dia memberiku susu dengan air untuk diminum. Dan dikatakan: Aku salat bersama jamaah. Dan dikatakan: Istri si fulan bersamanya. Dalam contoh pertama, bercampur dan menyatu tentu dipahami, sedangkan dalam contoh kedua, berbagi tempat dan perbuatan yang sama harus dipahami, tanpa bercampur; dan dalam contoh ketiga, menemani harus dipahami, meskipun mereka mungkin tidak berbagi tempat atau perbuatan yang sama. Setelah jelas bahwa makna kebersamaan dengan sesuatu atau seseorang berbeda-beda menurut apa yang disandarkan kepadanya, dan tidak mungkin dikatakan bahwa ia mengharuskan bercampur dan menyatu, atau berbagi tempat yang sama, karena hal itu mustahil disandarkan kepada Allah Yang Mahakuasa lagi Maha Perkasa, mengingat telah ditetapkan bahwa Dia sama sekali tidak serupa dengan mereka dan bahwa Dia Mahatinggi di atas mereka. Oleh karena itu, Dia bersama kita dan Dia di atas 'Arsy-Nya, di atas langit, karena Dia meliputi kita dengan ilmu-Nya, kekuasaan-Nya, otoritas-Nya, pendengaran-Nya, penglihatan-Nya, dan pengaturan-Nya terhadap urusan makhluk serta hal-hal lain yang diharuskan oleh Rububiyyah-Nya. Maka, jika seorang penafsir menjelaskannya sebagai bermakna ilmu, ia tidak melampaui apa yang diharuskan olehnya dan ia tidak bersalah melakukan ta'wil, kecuali, bagaimanapun juga, bagi mereka yang memahami kebersamaan dengan seseorang atau sesuatu sebagai bermakna berbagi tempat yang sama atau bercampur dan menyatu. Dan kami telah menyatakan bahwa hal ini tidak selalu demikian dalam setiap keadaan. Inilah mengenai apa yang diriwayatkan dari Imam Ahmad tentang (dugaan) ta'wil terhadap tiga dalil tersebut.

Fatawa tentang Aqidah 109 Badal! g glcd

Adapun melihat pada nash-nash itu sendiri, kami telah menyebutkan di atas bahwa tidak ada ta'wil dalam ayat yang mulia tersebut, jika seorang penafsir menjelaskannya sebagai bermakna ilmu, karena hal itu adalah bagian dari apa yang tentu harus dipahami darinya, bukan bentuk penyimpangan maknanya dari apa yang harus dipahami darinya.

Adapun hadith:

"Sesungguhnya hati anak-anak Adam berada di antara dua Jari dari jari-jari

Yang Maha Pengasih, bagaikan satu hati, dan Dia melakukan terhadap

mereka apa yang Dia kehendaki." Hadith ini diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Sahih-nya, dalam Kitab Al-Qadar pada bab ketiga, nomor 17, halaman 2045; dan tidak ada ta'wil di dalamnya, menurut Ahl as-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah, karena mereka menetapkan keyakinan terhadap Jari-jari Allah Ta'ala dengan cara yang layak bagi-Nya, dan kenyataan bahwa hati kita berada di antara dua Jari tidak mengharuskan bahwa jari-jari tersebut menyentuh hati. Ini karena awan berada di antara langit dan bumi, dan ia tidak menyentuh langit maupun bumi; demikian pula hati anak-anak Adam berada di antara dua Jari dari jari-jari Yang Maha Pengasih dan hal itu tidak mengharuskan bahwa jari-jari tersebut menyentuhnya.

Adapun hadith: "Hajar Aswad adalah Tangan Kanan Allah di bumi."

Syaikhul Islam Ibnu Taimiyyah berkata mengenainya dalam Al-Fatawa (6:397) dalam kumpulan Ibnu Qasim: Hadith ini telah diriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ dengan sanad yang tidak sahih, dan yang masyhur adalah bahwa ia berasal dari Ibnu Abbas, semoga Allah

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 110 PLY OS! sats

meridai keduanya, yang berkata: 'Hajar Aswad adalah Tangan Kanan Allah di bumi, maka barangsiapa menyentuhnya dan menciumnya, seolah-olah ia telah berjabat Tangan Kanan Allah dan menciumnya.' Maka beliau membatasinya sebagai di bumi dan bukan di setiap tempat, dan beliau berkata: 'Tangan Kanan Allah di bumi,' dan hukum lafaz yang terbatas berbeda dari sesuatu yang umum dan tidak terbatas. Dan beliau berkata: 'maka barangsiapa menyentuhnya dan menciumnya, seolah-olah ia telah berjabat Tangan Kanan Allah dan menciumnya.' Dan sudah diketahui bahwa serupa dengan sesuatu tidaklah sama dengan identik dengannya.

Saya katakan: Dengan demikian, hadith tersebut tidak berbicara tentang Sifat-sifat Allah Ta'ala yang maknanya telah diubah dari makna zahirnya, oleh karena itu tidak ada ta'wil di dalamnya sama sekali.

Adapun klaim mereka bahwa ada dua mazhab: Salah satunya dikenal sebagai mazhab Ibnu Taimiyyah, kami katakan bahwa penisbatan mazhab ini kepada Ibnu Taimiyyah adalah untuk mengklaim bahwa tidak ada seorang pun sebelum beliau yang mengatakan hal-hal ini. Ini keliru, karena keyakinan yang dipegang oleh Ibnu Taimiyyah adalah keyakinan yang dipegang oleh Salaf yang saleh dan para imam umat (Islam), dan bukan beliau yang memperkenalkan mazhab ini, sebagaimana diklaim oleh orang yang mengatakan demikian, yang bermaksud dengan itu untuk meremehkan pentingnya mazhab tersebut. Dan Allah-lah tempat memohon pertolongan.

Adapun sikap kami terhadap para ulama yang melakukan ta'wil, kami katakan: Siapa pun di antara mereka yang diketahui memiliki niat baik dan yang berkontribusi positif terhadap agama serta mengikuti Sunnah, maka ia dimaafkan, karena ia melakukan ta'wil berdasarkan suatu alasan (yang ia pandang dapat dibenarkan). Namun, dimaafkannya ia tidak berarti bahwa jalannya tidak keliru, yaitu metode yang bertentangan dengan metode Salaf yang saleh yang memahami nash-nash tersebut menurut makna zahirnya, dan meyakini apa yang ditunjukkan oleh makna zahir tersebut, tanpa berusaha menjelaskan bagaimananya, dan tanpa menyerupakannya.

Adalah wajib untuk membedakan antara hukum atas suatu perkataan dan hukum atas orang yang mengatakannya, serta antara suatu perbuatan dan orang yang melakukannya. Maka, jika perkataan yang keliru muncul dari hasil ijtihad dan niat baik, orang yang mengatakannya tidak tercela karenanya. Bahkan ia mendapat pahala atas ijtihadnya, berdasarkan sabda Nabi ﷺ:

SESE 5S ty col sh AB oul SF aged ass (S&S tip

oi i Bi id "Apabila seorang hakim memutuskan suatu perkara lalu ia berijtihad (dalam hal itu), dan ia benar, maka ia mendapat dua pahala; tetapi apabila ia memutuskan suatu perkara lalu ia berijtihad (dalam hal itu) dan ia keliru, maka ia mendapat satu pahala."

Adapun mengenai ia disifatkan sebagai sesat, jika yang dimaksudkan adalah bahwa ia sepenuhnya sesat, yang karenanya orang yang disifatkan demikian dipersalahkan dan dibenci, maka sifat ini tidak diterapkan kepada Mujtahid ini yang diketahui memiliki niat baik, memberikan kontribusi positif terhadap agama, dan mengikuti Sunnah.

Namun jika yang dimaksudkan dengan sesat adalah bahwa pendapatnya bertentangan dengan pandangan yang benar, tanpa bermaksud mencela orang yang memegangnya, maka tidak ada keberatan terhadap hal itu, karena orang seperti ini tidak sepenuhnya sesat. Dalam hal metodenya, ia benar, karena ia telah berusaha semaksimal kemampuannya untuk sampai pada kebenaran, tetapi dalam hal hasil ijtihadnya, ia keliru, karena ia telah menyelisihi kebenaran.

"1 Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Berpegang Teguh pada Al-Kitab (Allah) dan Sunnah, dalam Bab: Pahala Hakim yang Berijtihad dan Ia Benar atau Salah (7352) dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Peradilan, dalam Bab: Penjelasan tentang Pahala Hakim Ketika Ia Berijtihad (1716).

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 112 PEM ols i cst

Dan dengan penjelasan yang terperinci ini, keraguan dan kekhawatiran pun terangkat. Dan Allah-lah yang dimintai pertolongan.

Q. 30. Apakah keyakinan Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah mengenai Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah? Dan apa perbedaan antara Nama dan Sifat? Dan apakah menetapkan Nama mengharuskan penetapan Sifat? Dan apakah menetapkan Sifat mengharuskan penetapan Nama?

A. Keyakinan Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah mengenai Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat Allah adalah menetapkan Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat yang telah Allah tetapkan bagi Diri-Nya sendiri, tanpa mengubah maknanya, tanpa menafikannya, tanpa menjelaskan bagaimana caranya, dan tanpa menyerupakannya dengan makhluk.
content_ar: As for citing as the evidence the words of Ibn Al-Jawzi on this subject, we say: The sayings of the العلماء may be cited as evidence or evidence may be cited against them, and the saying of one scholar is not an evidence against the rest of them. As for their claim that الإمام Ahmad made Ta‘wil in الحديث النبوي الشريف: WSN lal by gtd 35 6st Lg Syn “Verily, the hearts ‘of the sons of Adam are between two of the Fingers of the Most Beneficent.” And الحديث النبوي الشريف: (oN Gb ail fa 55 Sed “The Black Stone is the right Hand of الله in the earth.” and the Words of الله, the Most High: Sak ch Bee ae Ek CG KE Bs ‘And He is with you (by His Knowledge) wheresoever you may be. mee] We say: It has not been authentically reported from الإمام Ahmad, رحمه الله, that he made Ta’wil of the above-mentioned two Ahadith. الفتاوى on ‘Aqgidah (Creed) 107 Beda! cul Shaikhul-الإسلام ابن تيمية, رحمه الله, said in Al-الفتاوى (5:398) in the collection of Ibn Qasim: As for what was reported by Abu Hamid الغزالي, that الإمام Ahmad did not make Ta’wil except in three things: (250 pal asi te 351 el “The Black Stone is the right Hand of الله in the earth.” and: tgAS3I lal by otel og oti Sth “The hearts of the sons of Adam are between two of the Fingers of the Most Beneficent.” tad ds ell ons bei eo! “Verily, I find the Spirit of the Most Beneficent in the direction of Yemen.” This report is a lie against Ahmad; no one has نقل it from him with an صحيح chain of narrators, and it is not known that any of his الصحابة نقل it from him. As for the Words of الله, the Most High: ‘And He is with you (by His nevi, wheresoever you may be. rl) الإمام Ahmad did not make Ta’wil of it; he only explained it by one of its essential attributes, which is Knowledge, in reply to the Jahmiyyah,'?! who explained it in a manner which contradicted its true meaning, claiming that it necessitates the belief () Al-Hadid 57:4 '?] Jahmiyyah: Named after its founder, Jahm bin Safwan, the Jahmiyyah movement denied all of the Names and Attributes of الله. that الله, the Most High is in every place in His Person Exalted is الله above what they claim. So he, رحمه الله, explained that His being with them here means that He encompasses the creation by His Knowledge of them. This is because being with something or someone does not necessitate its physical presence or mixing with it. Rather it must be understood in each place according to its context; this is why we say: He gave me milk with water to drink. And it is said: I prayed with the congregation. And it is said: So-and-so’s wife is with him. In the first example, associating and mixing is necessarily understood, while in the second example, sharing the same place and the same deed must be understood, without mixing; and in the third example, accompanying must be understood, even though they may not share the same place or action. Once it is clear that the meaning of being with something or someone differs according to what is annexed to it, and it is not possible to say that it necessitates associating and mixing, or sharing the same place, because it is impossible to attribute that to الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, due to the fact that it is confirmed that He is in no way similar to them and the fact that He is far above them. Therefore, He is with us and He is over His Throne, above the heavens, because He encompasses us with His Knowledge, His Omnipotence, His Authority, His Hearing, His Seeing and His Organizing the affairs of the creation and other matters necessitated by His Lordship. So, if a commentator explained it as meaning Knowledge, he has not exceeded what is necessitated by it and he is not thereby guilty of Ta‘wil, except, at any rate, to those who understand being with someone or something as meaning sharing the same place or associating and mixing. And we have already said that this is not necessarily so in all cases. This regards what has been نقل from الإمام Ahmad concerning the (supposed) Ta’wil of these three evidences. الفتاوى on ‘Agidah (Creed) 109 Badal! g glcd As for looking at the texts themselves, we have mentioned above that there is no Ta’wil in the Noble Verse, if a commentator explained it as meaning knowledge, because that is a part of what must necessarily be understood by it, it is not a form of distorting its meaning from what must be understood by it. As for الحديث النبوي الشريف: “Verily, the hearts of the sons of Adam are between two of the Fingers of the Most Beneficent are like one heart and He does with them as He wills.” It was روى by المسلم in his Sahih, in the Book of Al-Qadar in the third Chapter, number 17, page 2045; and there is no Ta'wil in it, according to Ahl as-السنة wal-Jama‘ah, since they confirm belief in the Fingers of الله, the Most High in a manner befitting Him, and the fact that our hearts are between two Fingers does not necessitate that they are touching the hearts. This is because the clouds are held between the heavens and the earth and they neither touch the sky nor the earth; likewise, the hearts of the sons of Adam are between two Fingers of the Most Beneficent and it does not necessitate that they are touching them. As for الحديث النبوي الشريف: “The Black Stone is the right Hand of الله in the earth.” Shaikhul-الإسلام ابن تيمية said regarding it in Al-الفتاوى, (6:397) in the compilation of Ibn Qasim: It has been روى from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #¢ with an unauthentic chain of narrators and what is well known is that it is from Ibn ‘Abbas, may الله be الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 110 PLY OS! sats pleased with them, who said: ‘The Black Stone is the right Hand of الله in the earth, so whoever touched it and kissed it, it is as if he had shaken الله’s right Hand and kissed it.’ So he restricted it to being on earth and not in every place and he says: ‘the right Hand of الله in the earth,’ and the ruling on a restricted wording is different from something general and unrestricted. And he said: ‘so whoever touched it and kissed it, it is as if he had shaken الله’s right Hand and kissed it.’ And it is well known that being similar to something is not the same as being identical to it. I say: Accordingly, الحديث النبوي الشريف is not speaking of the Attributes of الله, the Most High whose meanings have been changed from the apparent meanings, therefore there is no Ta’wil in it at all. As for their claim that there are two schools: One of them known as the school of ابن تيمية, we say that the attribution of this school to ابن تيمية is in order to claim that no one before him said these things. This is wrong, because the beliefs held by ابن تيمية are those which were held by the righteous Salaf and the الأئمة of the (المسلم) nation, and it was not he who introduced this school, as claimed by the one who says this, and who wishes thereby to minimize its importance and الله is the One Who is asked for Help. As for our position regarding those العلماء who made Ta’‘wil, we say: Any of them who was known to have a good intention and who contributed positively to the religion and who followed السنة النبوية is excused, due to his having exercised Ta’wil for some reason (which he saw as justifiable). However, his being excused does not mean that his path was not wrong, that is the method contrary to that of the righteous Salaf which was to understand them according to their apparent meaning, and to believe what that apparent meaning proved, without trying to explain how, and without comparison. It is necessary to differentiate between the ruling on a saying, and on the one who said it, and between a deed and the one who performed it. So, if an incorrect statement emanates from the exercise of الاجتهاد and a good intention, then the one who said it is not blamed for it. Indeed, he has a reward for his Tjtihad, according to the statement of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #: SESE 5S ty col sh AB oul SF aged ass (S&S tip oi i Bi id “If a judge makes a ruling and he exercises الاجتهاد (therein), and he is correct, then he has two rewards; but if he makes a ruling and exercise الاجتهاد (therein) and he is wrong, then he has one reward.’"") But as for him being described as astray, if what is meant by it is that he is completely astray, for which the person so described is held blameworthy and which is hated, then this description is not applied to this Mujtahid'?) who is known to have had a good intention, contributed positively to the religion, and followed السنة النبوية. But if what is intended by being astray is that his opinion contradicts the correct view, without indicating thereby censure of the one who holds it, then there is no objection to that, because such a person as this is not completely astray. Regarding his method, he is correct, since he has striven to the utmost of his ability to arrive at the truth, but as regards the result (of his الاجتهاد) he is astray, since he has contradicted the truth. And الله is the One Who is asked. Q. 30. What is the belief of Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah . regarding the Names and Attributes of الله? And what is the difference between a Name and an Attribute? And does confirming the Name necessitate confirming the Attribute? And does confirming the Attribute necessitate confirming the Name? A. The belief of Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah regarding the Names and Attributes of الله is to confirm the Names and Attributes which الله has confirmed for Himself, without distorting the meaning, without negating it, with explaining how and without comparison.
